chapters.title: Why TVL Exists
chapters.description: Why tuned variables and governed optimization are required for modern AI systems under drift.

sections.title: Design Goals at a Glance
sections.content: TVL keeps optimization governable by making tunable knobs, constraints, and promotion evidence explicit in one contract.

sections.title: Running Example · Campus Orientation RAG
sections.content: We revisit a retrieval-augmented system that answers student questions throughout the book. The workload is quirky: traffic spikes during move-in, gateway latency shifts during registration surges, and provider prices drift over time. This TVL file is the baseline we will extend.

sections.title: Field Notes Legend
sections.content: The book uses recurring callouts to separate fast tips, reliability gotchas, and integration guidance.

chapters.title: Getting Fluent in TVL
chapters.description: A practical TVL walkthrough from first module to repeatable validation and hands-on intuition.

sections.title: Minimal Working Spec
sections.content: Here is the smallest useful TVL module. Read it top to bottom and notice how every field supports the optimizer.

sections.title: Validation Warm-Up
sections.content: Use tvl-validate to catch missing sections, schema mismatches, and other cheap mistakes before you optimize.

sections.title: TVL Type System
sections.content: TVL enforces explicit typing on every tuned variable so search spaces stay interpretable and safe.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
sections.content: The most common early mistakes are wrong type names, missing promotion policy, and constraint syntax borrowed from other languages.

sections.title: Analog Circuit Lab · Explore the Knobs
sections.content: Try the Orientation RAG micro simulation to feel each tuning variable. The sliders map directly to the spec above; latency, cost, and quality update in real time, and constraint warnings match the rules we enforce in Chapter 3.

chapters.title: Constraints, Units, and Safety Nets
chapters.description: Typed structural rules, operational preconditions, and satisfiability preflights for safe exploration.

sections.title: Two Kinds of Constraints
sections.content: TVL separates static structural rules from environment-scoped operational preconditions so authors know what is validated when.

sections.title: Structural Constraint Syntax
sections.content: Structural constraints use typed DNF formulas over TVARs. The syntax differs from general-purpose languages:.

sections.title: Operational Preconditions
sections.content: Operational preconditions in TVL are written under `constraints.derived` and checked against the current environment snapshot.

sections.title: Analog Circuit Drill · Watch Constraints React
sections.content: Load the Orientation RAG lab (Latency Spike preset) to see constraint feedback the moment a slider moves.

sections.title: Testing Constraints
sections.content: Use tvl-check-structural and unit tests to prove the search space still contains valid configurations.

sections.title: Checklist Before Promotion
sections.content: Before promotion, confirm the search space is satisfiable, the formulas use TVL syntax, and operational preconditions only reference environment symbols.

chapters.title: Patterns for Real Deployments
chapters.description: Composition, overlays, safe narrowing, and provenance patterns for production operations.

sections.title: Module Composition with tvl-compose
sections.content: The TVL core schema stays standalone and self-contained, so overlays are handled through preprocessing rather than inheritance in the schema itself.

sections.title: Layered Specifications
sections.content: Base modules stay fully valid, while overlays capture environment-specific narrowing before composition.

sections.title: Visualizing Overlays
sections.content: Each composed output is a valid, standalone TVL module. Overlays are inputs to the composition process, not runtime artifacts.

sections.title: Override Rules
sections.content: tvl-compose enforces safe narrowing so overlays can tighten risk without silently widening the contract.

sections.title: Hotfix Playbook
sections.content: When an incident hits, create a short-lived overlay instead of mutating the base module in place.

sections.title: Provenance Matters
sections.content: Every promotion should capture the exact composed spec, overlay inputs, and replay metadata needed for audits.

chapters.title: Integration Patterns
chapters.description: How TVL plugs into CI, optimizer runtime, data validation, and auditable promotion flows.

sections.title: TVL CLI Tools
sections.content: The TVL toolchain provides focused commands for local validation, CI gates, and operational checks.

sections.title: Deterministic Artifacts
sections.content: Every TVL workflow should leave behind deterministic artifacts so decisions stay reproducible and reviewable.

sections.title: Field Notes · Replay the Optimization Story
sections.content: The Orientation RAG lab includes the same presets we evaluate in CI. Jump between Baseline , Latency Spike , and Budget Shift to see how TVO trades objectives while DVL enforces guardrails.

sections.title: Manifests and Promotion
sections.content: After a successful optimization run, the manifest becomes the audit record for what changed, why it won, and whether rollout is safe.

chapters.title: Training Agents with Tuned Variables
chapters.description: Turn agent design choices into explicit TVARs, evaluation budgets, and promotion rules instead of leaving them buried in prompts and orchestration code.

sections.title: From Runtime Patterns to Training Surfaces
sections.content: Pattern names are the beginning of the design discussion; the real engineering work starts when those patterns are translated into an explicit, governable control surface.

sections.title: Choosing Tuned Variables for Agent Systems
sections.content: Good TVARs correspond to real architectural choices, observable trade-offs, and repeatable evaluation slices rather than to every prompt token a team could edit.

sections.title: Budgets, Feedback, and Promotion Loops
sections.content: Agent tuning becomes maintainable only when the exploration budget, evaluation feedback, and promotion rule are designed together instead of as separate afterthoughts.

chapters.title: Pattern Anatomy and Evaluation
chapters.description: Learn to compare agent patterns as governed control surfaces rather than as isolated prompt recipes or one-off demos.

sections.title: How to Read a Pattern Control Surface
sections.content: The fastest way to compare patterns is to read each one as a control surface: tuned variables, decision axes, failure modes, and the evidence needed for promotion.

sections.title: Batching, Map-Reduce, and Routed Expertise
sections.content: These breadth-oriented patterns all spend more budget on coverage, but they do it through very different control surfaces and failure modes.

sections.title: Reflection, Judges, and Committee Decisions
sections.content: Depth-oriented refinement and committee-style decision patterns both add reasoning cost, but they do it for different purposes and must be evaluated with different evidence.

chapters.title: Composing Patterns for Production
chapters.description: Combine agent patterns only when they improve the study plane, not just because each scaffold looked attractive in isolation.

sections.title: Scaffold, Controller, and Bundle Logic
sections.content: The cleanest multi-pattern systems separate the stable scaffold from the controller that adds extra search, reflection, or judging behavior.

sections.title: Safe Composition Rules
sections.content: Pattern bundles should narrow or discipline the operational surface unless there is strong evidence that a broader bundle is still governable.

sections.title: When to Stop Adding Patterns
sections.content: A pattern stack should stop growing when the next controller increases complexity faster than it improves the governed operating point.
